title: House Armed Services draft bill eliminates SDA, Space RCO as separate entities
summary: A draft bill by the House Armed Services Committee (HASC) for the 2027 NDAA proposes eliminating the Space Development Agency (SDA) and Space Rapid Capabilities Office (Space RCO) as separate entities. The bill supports their realignment under a portfolio-based acquisition structure.

title: Mars to Titan: the next rallying call?
summary: The article discusses the future of human space exploration beyond Mars, questioning whether Mars should be a steppingstone to other destinations like Titan. It explores the idea of expanding human presence in the solar system after establishing a foothold on Mars.

title: European space industry warns EU Space Act could slow competitiveness
summary: European space industry representatives and legal experts express concerns that the EU Space Act's bureaucratic and rigid nature may hinder competitiveness. The criticism was raised during SmallSat Europe.

title: As geopolitics reshape space, SpinLaunch sees an opening
summary: SpinLaunch's CEO highlights how geopolitics is reshaping the space industry, creating opportunities for the company. The shifting landscape is driven by sovereignty concerns and strategic interests alongside technological advancements.

title: Kongsberg NanoAvionics sets sights on building and supporting sovereign constellations
summary: Kongsberg NanoAvionics, a satellite manufacturer, secured a €122.5 million contract to build 280 satellites for sovereign constellations. The company, established in 2014, is expanding its focus from individual satellites to large-scale constellation support.

title: Former Ukraine official calls for stricter restrictions on Russian use of Starlink
summary: A former Ukrainian defense official urged SpaceX to impose stricter controls on Starlink terminals, alleging they are being used by Russian forces via third-party countries. The call highlights concerns over the unintended military use of Starlink technology.

title: Sitael Accelerates Growth in Space: New Missions, Industrial Capacity and a Trajectory Towards 2031
summary: Sitael has announced a growth plan targeting €200 million in revenues by 2031, supported by over €150 million in backlog and nine planned launches by 2030. The company is expanding its industrial capacity and mission portfolio.

title: Schaeffler AG joins forces with Spire to build sovereign European satellites
summary: Schaeffler AG has partnered with Spire Global to enhance satellite production in Germany, aiming to build sovereign European satellites. This collaboration strengthens Europe's space capabilities through a major German automotive systems manufacturer.

title: Starcloud’s path to 88,000 computing satellites
summary: Starcloud launched its first satellite, Starcloud-1, aboard a SpaceX mission in November, featuring an NVIDIA H100 compute system. The company aims to deploy a massive constellation of 88,000 computing satellites.

title: Spire expands European manufacturing with Munich plant
summary: Spire Global has opened a new manufacturing facility in Munich to produce up to 100 small satellites annually. This expansion adds to Spire's existing plants in Boulder, Colorado, and Glasgow, Scotland.

title: Russian cosmonauts install sun-watching telescope on ISS during 6-hour spacewalk
summary: Russian cosmonauts Sergey Kud-Sverchkov and Sergei Mikaev installed a sun-watching telescope on the ISS during a 6-hour spacewalk. They also retrieved science experiments while outside the International Space Station.

title: China shakes up its space programs to land astronauts on the moon by 2030: 'We will spare no effort'
summary: China is integrating its robotic lunar probe missions with its human spaceflight program to accelerate plans for landing astronauts on the moon by 2030. The country's space agency has pledged to prioritize this goal with full commitment.

title: FAA grounds SpaceX's Starship V3 megarocket after Flight 12 'mishap'
summary: The FAA has grounded SpaceX's Starship V3 megarocket after Flight 12 experienced a mishap, requiring an investigation before it can fly again.

title: What is Star City, the secret Cold War cosmonaut training town in Apple TV's new 'For All Mankind' spinoff?
summary: Apple TV's 'For All Mankind' spinoff explores Star City, a secret Cold War cosmonaut training town. The town was a highly classified Soviet facility used for space mission preparations.

title: Every Lego Star Wars set released in 2026 that I wish I had
summary: Lego announced an extensive lineup of Star Wars-themed sets for 2026, featuring interactive Smart Brick-enabled models and detailed designs like the AT-AT. The collection is highly anticipated by fans and collectors.

title: 3 planets will line up in the night sky tonight: Here's where to look for the mini planetary parade
summary: Jupiter, Venus, and Mercury will align in the western sky at sunset tonight, creating a rare planetary parade visible to the naked eye.

title: Laser beams blast through the cosmos | Space photo of the day for May 27, 2026
summary: Laser beams are used for scientific purposes in space, as highlighted in a space photo from May 27, 2026. The beams are not a threat but part of ongoing research.

title: NASA will reveal the Artemis 3 astronauts on June 9
summary: NASA will announce the Artemis 3 astronauts on June 9. The crew will fly on the Artemis 3 docking mission in Earth orbit next year.

title: 'One of the great unresolved embarrassments of physics': It's been 340 years since Newton and scientists still haven't solved the secret of gravity
summary: Scientists have yet to precisely define the gravitational constant 'Big G' since Newton's time 340 years ago. A researcher recently made progress by opening a single envelope in their decade-long quest to solve this physics mystery.

title: The Milky Way may have devoured another galaxy named Loki, and astronomers think they've found its remains
summary: Astronomers believe the Milky Way may have absorbed a dwarf galaxy named 'Loki' and identified 20 stars that likely originated from it. The remnants of this ancient galactic merger have been detected within our galaxy.

title: NASA Develops Sensor to Improve Firefighter Safety
summary: NASA's FireSense project developed low-cost thermal sensors for fire bulldozers to alert firefighters of dangerous heat levels during wildfires. The sensors also provide critical data on fire behavior beneath the canopy.

title: Released: NASA Goddard Issues Draft Request for Proposal for the Landsat 10 Spacecraft
summary: NASA Goddard has issued a Draft Request for Proposal (DRFP) for the Landsat 10 spacecraft, available for review on SAM.gov.

title: La NASA ofrece información actualizada sobre rovers, módulos de alunizaje y misiones de Base Lunar
summary: NASA provided updates on rovers, lunar landers, and Moon Base missions during a briefing on the Lunar Base program. The agency announced new contracts for crewed lunar vehicles and uncrewed cargo landers destined for the Moon.

title: Girl Scouts Event Brings Space Science to the Next Generation
summary: NASA engaged young girls in space science through hands-on activities and experiments in May 2026. The event aimed to inspire the next generation of scientists in heliophysics.

title: La NASA anunciará la tripulación de Artemis III e informará sobre el progreso de la misión
summary: NASA will announce the crew for Artemis III and provide updates on the mission's progress during a live event on June 9. The announcement will take place at the Johnson Space Center in Houston.

title: Students Build Moon Robots for NASA’s 2026 Lunabotics Challenge
summary: Students from around the U.S. are building lunar robots for NASA's 2026 Lunabotics Challenge. The competition will test the robots' ability to navigate difficult lunar terrain.

title: NASA-European Sea Level Mission Homes in on El Niño
summary: NASA and European partners' satellite data reveals a large warm water swell in the Pacific Ocean, indicating a likely El Niño later this year. The mission aims to track sea level changes linked to climate patterns.

title: Webinar 6/17: Discover, Access, and Task Commercial Data with NASA’s Satellite Data Explorer
summary: NASA is hosting a webinar on June 17 to demonstrate how to use the Satellite Data Explorer for searching, accessing, and tasking commercial Earth Observation data. This event focuses on enabling users to discover and utilize commercial satellite data effectively.

title: NASA’s Webb Reveals Black Hole That Formed Before Its Galaxy
summary: NASA's James Webb Space Telescope discovered a black hole that formed before its host galaxy, challenging the traditional understanding of black hole and galaxy formation. This finding suggests that black holes may have played a role in shaping galaxies rather than the other way around.

title: National Institutes of Health Nutrition Education Challenge
summary: NASA's Center of Excellence for Collaborative Innovation (CoECI) is assisting the National Institutes of Health (NIH) in a nutrition education challenge through its NASA Tournament Lab. The challenge invites U.S.-based academic institutions to participate in crowdsourced initiatives.

title: Rovers and Drones laying the foundations for NASA Moon Base
summary: NASA has outlined initial missions for its Moon Base initiative, utilizing rovers and drones to lay the groundwork. The initiative involves rebranding existing Commercial Lunar Payload Services (CLPS) missions.

title: Following Starship V3 debut, SpaceX prepares for follow up
summary: SpaceX successfully conducted the first flight of Starship Version 3 this week. The company is now preparing for a follow-up mission.

title: Launch Preview: Starlink and Amazon Leo missions fill manifest
summary: Six orbital launches are scheduled this week, with four Falcon 9 Starlink missions and Amazon's Leo satellite missions leading the manifest. The launches highlight the growing activity in commercial spaceflight and satellite deployment.

title: Vehicle arrivals, science experiments, and maintenance keep Expedition 74 busy through April
summary: Expedition 74 crew aboard the International Space Station has been occupied with vehicle arrivals, science experiments, and maintenance tasks throughout April. The activities highlight ongoing operations and research on the ISS.

title: Shenzhou 23 launches new crew to Tiangong space station
summary: Shenzhou 23 successfully launched a new crew to the Tiangong space station, which has been permanently occupied since June 5, 2022. This mission marks another step in China's ongoing space station operations.

title: Starship Flight 12: Block 3 Impresses on First Flight
summary: Starship Flight 12, featuring Block 3 upgrades, successfully launched on May 22, 2026, from SpaceX's facilities. The mission demonstrated significant improvements in performance and reliability.

title: Launch Preview: Falcon 9, Electron, and Vega C launch missions alongside Starship Flight 12
summary: Six space launches are scheduled globally in the next week, including SpaceX's 12th Starship flight, a Falcon 9 mission, an Electron launch, and a Vega C flight.

title: Vega C launches SMILE to study Earth’s magnetosphere
summary: ESA's SMILE mission launched aboard Vega C to study Earth's magnetosphere. The mission aims to investigate the interaction between solar wind and Earth's magnetic field.

title: Super Heavy Block 3 the Booster of the Future
summary: The Super Heavy Block 3 booster, part of SpaceX's Starship program, has undergone significant upgrades. This new iteration represents the future of the booster design.

title: Artemis III deep into processing and planning for key HLS test
summary: NASA is actively planning and processing the Artemis III mission, which is a critical crewed mission to the Moon. The mission includes key Human Landing System (HLS) tests.

title: TEC Business to Business website
summary: The TEC Business to Business website reported on space industry news, but no specific details were provided in the given input.

title: ESA Delivers: 50 years booklet
summary: ESA celebrates its 50th anniversary with a booklet highlighting 50 key achievements over the past five decades. The publication underscores the agency's milestones and contributions to space exploration and technology.

title: ESA’s Prodex programme brings scientific research to space
summary: ESA's Prodex programme facilitates scientific research in space, with its latest success being the 4DSpace-Daedalus mission launched in Norway. The programme supports research institutes in participating in European space science activities and missions.

title: Stay space chemical compliant in the EU
summary: The European Space Agency (ESA) will host a free REACH workshop on chemical compliance in the space sector on 2 June at its technical centre in the Netherlands, with an online attendance option available.

title: The young minds space-proofing ESA’s missions
summary: Researchers in ESA's Materials, Environments and Contamination Control Section test materials to ensure they survive space conditions, supported by young, innovative minds.

title: Extended Reality at ESA opens new pathways for space exploration
summary: The European Space Agency (ESA) is leveraging Extended Reality (XR) for training, operations, simulations, and public engagement in space exploration. This initiative aims to enhance efficiency and accessibility in space activities.

title: Sensitive and sturdy
summary: The news discusses a sturdy and sensitive component in the space industry. The image likely depicts this component.

title: The great parachute bake-out
summary: A space mission involving a parachute system underwent a critical thermal test to ensure its reliability during re-entry. The bake-out process simulated extreme conditions to validate the parachute's performance.

title: Baking a parachute for Mars
summary: ESA's ExoMars Rosalind Franklin rover mission is preparing a 35-meter parachute for Mars by sterilizing it to prevent microbial contamination. The parachute, the largest ever for Mars, will slow the rover's descent during its 2028 landing to search for signs of life.

title: A new grip on space: electrostatic capture technology
summary: A new electrostatic capture technology has been developed for space applications, enabling safer and more precise satellite docking and debris removal. This innovation could significantly enhance in-space servicing and assembly operations.

title: Aitech Upgrades its Space Supercomputer
summary: Aitech has upgraded its space supercomputer by integrating NVIDIA's IGX Thor platform into the S-A2300 COTS AI Supercomputer, significantly enhancing in-orbit data processing capabilities for customers.

title: NASA Awards LTV, Lunar Transport Contracts
summary: NASA has awarded contracts for the Lunar Terrain Vehicle (LTV) and lunar transport services, marking a significant step toward returning humans to the Moon. NASA Administrator Jared Isaacman announced the contracts during a press conference at NASA HQ.

title: Op-ed: Commercial Demand in LEO is Lacking. The Government Can Help.
summary: A recent op-ed argues that the most promising commercial opportunity in Low Earth Orbit (LEO)—in-space manufacturing—currently lacks sufficient demand to be sustainable, and calls for government support to drive growth.

title: Optical Links: When to Incorporate Them? When Not To?
summary: The article discusses the cost-effectiveness of optical links for satellite applications, noting that they often cost hundreds of thousands of dollars and may not be worth the investment for many use cases.

title: Satellogic Lands $18M+ Defense-Monitoring Contract
summary: Satellogic secured a defense-monitoring contract worth over $18 million, marking its first major agreement following a strategic pivot to persistent-monitoring capabilities.

title: SpaceX Launches Starship V3 for the First Time
summary: SpaceX successfully launched its Starship V3 rocket for the first time, marking its twelfth test flight and introducing a full suite of upgrades. This milestone represents a significant advancement in the development of the Starship program.

title: SEOPS Will Start Waymaker LEO Rideshares In 2028
summary: SEOPS is set to launch Waymaker LEO rideshares in 2028, targeting time-sensitive or non-standard payloads that current rideshare options cannot accommodate.

title: The US Needs a New Launch Site, Air Force Secretary Says
summary: The US Air Force Secretary has stated that existing military launch sites are nearing maximum capacity, necessitating a new launch site. This highlights the growing demand for space launch capabilities.

title: ESA Chief Calls on Europe to Double Funds for Space
summary: ESA's chief urges Europe to double its funding for space initiatives, arguing that current investments are insufficient despite recent growth in public funds for the sector.

title: Better Moon-Tech Tops NASA Stakeholder Wishlist
summary: NASA's stakeholder survey revealed that improved Moon technology is the top priority for space tech investments. The feedback was gathered two years ago to guide future development.

title: Will the European Launch Market Finally Ignite?
summary: The European space industry is seeking more efficient and reliable access to space, a topic that dominated a recent launch panel discussion. The question of when this will be achieved remains a key challenge for the region.

title: Archangel Lightworks Demonstrates Deployable Optical Ground Station
summary: Archangel Lightworks successfully tested its deployable optical ground station Terra-M, demonstrating data transfer with a LEO satellite during field trials. The company, based in [location not specified], showcased the system's capability to support satellite communications.

title: Anuvu Taps Eutelsat for IFC Capacity on Eutelsat 10B
summary: Anuvu has signed a multi-year agreement with Eutelsat to use satellite capacity on the Eutelsat 10B satellite for in-flight connectivity (IFC) services. The Eutelsat 10B satellite is positioned at 10 degrees East.

title: European Commission Reserves Portion of Critical MSS Spectrum for EU Operators
summary: The European Commission has outlined a process to allocate licenses for critical mobile satellite services (MSS) spectrum in Europe, prioritizing EU operators. This decision aims to regulate and secure spectrum usage for satellite communications within the region.

title: Space Force Awards SpaceX $2.3 Billion Contract for Space Data Network
summary: The U.S. Space Force awarded SpaceX a $2.29 billion contract to develop the Space Data Network (SDN) Backbone, a proliferated Low-Earth Orbit (LEO) system. This contract is part of the Space Systems Command's efforts to enhance space-based data infrastructure.

title: Rideshare Providers SEOPS and Exolaunch Buy Dedicated Falcon 9 Missions
summary: SEOPS and Exolaunch have purchased dedicated SpaceX Falcon 9 missions to run their own rideshare missions, as announced during Smallsat Europe.

title: Connected Car Market Picks Up Steam With Standards and NTN, Experts Say
summary: The connected car market is gaining momentum with the establishment of standards and Non-Terrestrial Networks (NTN), according to experts at SmallSat Europe. BMW Group's Olaf Eckart highlighted the industry's readiness to unlock its potential.

title: American Airlines to Equip More Than 500 Planes with Starlink Wi-Fi
summary: American Airlines has partnered with SpaceX to equip over 500 narrowbody Airbus aircraft with Starlink's in-flight Wi-Fi service. This deal expands the airline's connectivity options using SpaceX's satellite internet technology.

title: Space Force Awards Viasat and Intelsat General $438M for Continued Work on PTS-G
summary: The U.S. Space Force awarded Viasat and Intelsat General a $438 million contract for continued work on the Protected Tactical Satellite-Global (PTS-G) program. The contract involves satellite development under the Space Systems Command.

title: ST Engineering iDirect’s New CEO Kuppanna Talks Ground Tech’s Cloud-Native, Interoperable Future
summary: ST Engineering iDirect appointed Sridhar Kuppanna as its new CEO in late March. The company is focusing on cloud-native and interoperable ground technology solutions amid a dynamic market.
